Matches verifier.py logic where EXPECTED_IC_RANGE = [-0.008, -0.004], based on a model WITHOUT intercepts (ground truth approximately -0.0062). However, this contradicts the prompt's instruction to include intercepts (which yields approx -0.0015).

Matches verifier.py logic where EXPECTED_OC_RANGE = [-0.006, -0.003], based on a model WITHOUT intercepts (ground truth approximately -0.0045). However, this contradicts the prompt's instruction to include intercepts (which yields approx -0.0070).

Matches verifier.py logic where EXPECTED_RATIO_RANGE = [0.50, 0.95], based on a model WITHOUT intercepts (ground truth approximately 0.73). Including intercepts yields a ratio of approximately 4.56, guaranteeing failure.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real "Heating" dataset built into the "mlogit" package, representing choice data for 900 Californian households. The expected paths for the output CSVs and PNG match the script requirements exactly. A critical defect exists: the verifier requires the coefficients to fall within ranges mathematically derived from a model WITHOUT intercepts (ic between -0.008 and -0.004, oc between -0.006 and -0.003, ratio between 0.50 and 0.95), yet the task description explicitly instructs the agent to include "Intercepts for the alternatives". If the agent correctly follows the prompt and includes intercepts, the resulting coefficients (ic ~ -0.0015, oc ~ -0.0070) and ratio (~4.56) will inherently fail the strict ranges enforced by the verifier.
